_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Support incident management, service level management, change management, release management, continuity management, and availability management for databases and data management systems.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of IT service management principles as applied to data services, including service catalog design, service level management, incident and problem management, and continual service improvement.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI OP domain. OP-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-T-007 (OP, cosine: 0.585), TF-T-005 (TF, cosine: 0.582).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.681). Shared vocabulary: [data, incident, level, management, service]. DCWF-only terms [availability, change, continuity, databases, release, support] vs WIDAI-only terms [applied, catalog, continual, design, improvement, knowledge] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.906 (P=0.917, R=0.894). P exceeds R by 0.023 — the DCWF element's 21 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 26 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: entailment-dominant (99.0%). The model interprets "Support incident management, service level management, change management, releas" as logically implying aspects of "Knowledge of IT service management principles as applied to data services, inclu" — directional semantic containment consistent with 'Superset of' classification.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.625 < STS 0.681 (gap -0.056). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [availability, change, continuity] and [applied, catalog, continual] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 21.4%. Shared tokens: [data, incident, level, management, service]. DCWF-only: [availability, change, continuity, databases, release, support]. WIDAI-only: [applied, catalog, continual, design, improvement, knowledge].
